name: run-demo
_doc: Demonstrates the v0.9 run step: it executes an observable shell command (mkdir -p out) under a relative working_dir (scratch), snapshots the created directory, gates on the command's recorded exit code, and ends Completed_no_commit (no commit needed). The run step executes ONLY if the operator passes --allow-run mkdir at runtime (e.g. cabal-workflow-runner run examples/run-demo.workflow.json --allow-run mkdir); with no allowlist the run step is Blocked (fail-closed). working_dir bounds the cwd/snapshot scope but does NOT sandbox the command from absolute paths in its args — the allowlist is the trust control. Linting reports the expected informational warning run-step-executes-commands (no errors).
